name: git_status
description: Show the working tree status of a git repository. Returns the current branch, staged/unstaged changes, and untracked files. Use this to understand the current state of the repository before making changes.

path.description: Optional. Path to the git repository. If not provided, uses the current working directory.

name: git_diff
description: Show changes between commits, commit and working tree, etc. Shows unstaged changes by default. Use 'staged' parameter to see staged changes, or provide commit hashes to compare specific commits.

path.description: Optional. Path to the git repository. If not provided, uses the current working directory.

staged.description: If true, show staged changes (git diff --staged). Default is false (show unstaged changes).

commit1.description: Optional. First commit hash, branch name, or tag for comparison.

commit2.description: Optional. Second commit hash, branch name, or tag for comparison. If commit1 is provided without commit2, compares commit1 with HEAD.

file.description: Optional. Limit diff to a specific file path.

name: git_log
description: Show commit logs. Returns a formatted list of commits with hash, author, date, and message. Use this to understand the project history, find specific commits, or review recent changes.

path.description: Optional. Path to the git repository. If not provided, uses the current working directory.

count.description: Optional. Number of commits to show. Default is 20.

branch.description: Optional. Show log for a specific branch. Default is current branch.

file.description: Optional. Show log for a specific file only.

oneline.description: Optional. If true, show compact one-line format. Default is false (shows full format).

name: git_add
description: Add file contents to the staging area (index). Use this to stage files before committing. Can add individual files, directories, or use patterns.

path.description: Optional. Path to the git repository. If not provided, uses the current working directory.

files.description: Files or directories to stage. Can be individual file paths, directory paths, or patterns like '*.cpp'. Use '.' to stage all changes.

name: git_commit
description: Record changes to the repository. Creates a new commit with the staged changes and the given message.

path.description: Optional. Path to the git repository. If not provided, uses the current working directory.

name: git_branch
description: List, create, or delete branches. Without arguments, lists all local branches. Use 'name' to create a new branch. Use 'name' with 'delete=true' to delete a branch.

path.description: Optional. Path to the git repository. If not provided, uses the current working directory.

name.description: Optional. Name of the branch to create or delete. If not provided, lists all branches.

delete.description: Optional. If true, delete the specified branch. Use with 'name' parameter.

remote.description: Optional. If true, list remote branches instead of local branches.

name: git_checkout
description: Switch branches or restore working tree files. Use 'branch' to switch to a different branch. Use 'file' to restore a specific file to its committed state.

path.description: Optional. Path to the git repository. If not provided, uses the current working directory.

branch.description: Name of the branch to switch to.

file.description: Optional. Restore a specific file instead of switching branches. If provided, discards local changes to that file.

create.description: Optional. If true, create the branch before switching to it (git checkout -b <branch>).

name: git_init
description: Create an empty git repository or reinitialize an existing one. Creates a .git directory with all necessary files.

path.description: Path where the git repository should be initialized. If not provided, uses the current working directory.

bare.description: Optional. If true, create a bare repository (no working tree). Default is false.

name: git_clone
description: Clone a repository into a new directory. Downloads the entire repository including all branches and history.

url.description: The URL of the remote repository to clone (e.g., https://github.com/user/repo.git).

directory.description: Optional. The directory to clone into. If not provided, uses the repository name.

depth.description: Optional. Create a shallow clone with truncated history. Use 1 for only the latest commit.
